utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with required details and costs: 1) health_coverage: The format requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan provides separate physical_health and mental_health entries, includes plan names and features, and gives a combined total_health_cost (€165/month). Costs are present; details adequate. 2) property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides coverage description (replacement value, electronics up to €1000) and cost (€60/month). Adequate. 3) travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. The plan provides coverage description and cost (€50/month). Adequate. 4) additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists Premium Insurance Membership with benefits and cost €20/month (optional). Adequate. 5) total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan includes base_monthly_cost, with_premium_membership, current_cost, monthly_savings, annual_savings, savings_percentage. This is a complete breakdown including comparisons. Therefore, 5 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes the following items that match entries in the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health (€100/month) and LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health (€65/month) under primary health coverage; LifeFirst Standard Coverage for property/home contents (€60/month); LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel insurance (€50/month). It also lists a Premium Insurance Membership at €20/month, which corresponds to General Services: Premium Insurance Membership. I mapped each of these named items to their exact ratings from the ground-truth.
